evidence.scientific_clarity: Stacked bars for 4 classes across 5 conditions with y-axis 0–1 and percent labels exactly matches the brief.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 set; pdffonts confirms Type 3 (bitmap) DejaVu Sans — default font, not editable/selectable.
evidence.palette: Green (#6ACC65) and red (#D65F5F) assigned to categorical Class B and C, violating directional-color discipline; red-green pair is problematic for deuteranopia.
evidence.layout_density: 6×4 in figure, width=0.6 bars, tight_layout, no overlapping labels, y-range 0–1 with 25% gridlines — well-used space.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption names colors and makes specific trend claims; however green/red for categorical classes will likely draw reviewer comment about colorblind accessibility.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF embeds DejaVu Sans as Type 3 (not Type 42 TrueType); script never sets matplotlib.rcParams['pdf.fonttype']=42, so text is not selectable.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (11 kB), figure.png (23 kB), figure.svg (28 kB), caption.tex (363 B), gen_figure.py (1.4 kB) all present and non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption identifies all four classes with colors, states bars sum to 1, and makes specific directional claims (rising Class A, Class C peak in Cond2/Cond5).

overall_strengths: Clean, well-proportioned layout with proper percent y-axis, tight_layout, and all required output files present.
Caption is specific and informative, naming classes, colors, and concrete trends visible in the figure.
overall_weaknesses: Missing pdf.fonttype=42 (and svg.fonttype=0) means PDF/SVG text is bitmap Type 3, not selectable or editable — a critical omission for publication-ready figures.
Palette uses green and red for categorical classes, violating directional-color discipline and failing colorblind-safe requirements (deuteranopia red-green confusion).
